name: Electromagnetic Reconnaissance
description: Detection and assessment of foreign electromagnetic radiations in order to build the electromagnetic order of battle.

name: Threat Warning
description: Use of sensors to detect immediate threats and activate countermeasures.

name: Direction Finding
description: Procedures used to determine the location of emitters. Techniques such as lines of bearing, cuts, or fixes may be employed.

name: Electromagnetic Probing
description: Intentional introduction of electromagnetic radiation into adversary systems in order to learn their specific functions and operational capabilities.

name: Directed-Energy Weapons
description: Use of directed-energy weapons to damage, degrade, or destroy adversary sensors, electronic components, or electrical systems.

name: Lasers
description: Use of lasers to blind optical sensors or destroy components.

name: High-Power Microwave
description: Use of high-power microwave energy to disable electronic components.

name: Electromagnetic Pulse
description: Use of strong bursts of electromagnetic radiation to permanently damage electrical systems.

name: Electromagnetic Jamming
description: Deliberate radiation of electromagnetic energy to reduce the enemy's effective use of the electromagnetic spectrum.

name: Spot Jamming
description: Jamming concentrated on a single frequency.

name: Barrage Jamming
description: Simultaneous jamming of multiple frequencies across a wide frequency band.

name: Sweep Jamming
description: Rapid sweeping of power across a range of frequencies.

name: Follower Jamming
description: Passive jamming system that detects an enemy transmission and automatically attacks it.

name: Standoff Jamming
description: Jamming conducted from a protected stationary position within friendly areas in order to support an advance.

name: Escort Jamming
description: Use of jamming platforms that physically accompany maneuver forces.

name: CREW
description: Counter-radio-controlled improvised explosive device electronic warfare systems used to block radio-controlled explosive device detonators.

name: Electromagnetic Deception
description: Deliberate radiation of electromagnetic energy to transmit misleading information.

name: Simulative Deception
description: Creation of false emission profiles to simulate capabilities that do not actually exist.

name: Manipulative Deception
description: Use of real communication signals to transmit misleading indicators of friendly intentions.

name: Imitative Deception
description: Imitation of enemy emissions, such as entering their networks using their call signs, in order to cause confusion.

name: Electromagnetic Intrusion
description: Intentional insertion of electromagnetic energy into enemy transmission paths to deceive operators or generate confusion.

name: Meaconing
description: Reception of navigation signals and retransmission of those signals on the same frequency in order to confuse the enemy.

name: Defensive Electromagnetic Attack
description: Use of defensive electromagnetic attack measures for self-protection, including decoys, flares, chaff, and counter-radio-controlled explosive device systems.

name: Decoys
description: Use of self-propelled, towed, or stationary decoys to divert attacks.

name: Flares or Chaff
description: Use of flares and chaff to confuse enemy missiles and radars.

name: Hardening
description: Filtering, shielding, and grounding actions used to protect systems against electromagnetic pulse or directed-energy weapons.

name: EMCON
description: Selective and controlled use of emitters to optimize command and control while minimizing detection by enemy sensors.

name: Radio Discipline
description: Use of the minimum necessary transmission power and minimization of transmission time through pre-planned messages and brevity codes.

name: Terrain Masking
description: Tactical positioning of antennas behind terrain features or buildings to avoid enemy detection.

name: Electromagnetic Masking
description: Controlled radiation on friendly frequencies to conceal real emissions.

name: Frequency Hopping
description: Rapid changes of frequency to make enemy interception and jamming more difficult.

name: Spread Spectrum
description: Deliberate variation of frequency to reduce susceptibility to interference.

name: Wartime Reserve Modes
description: Operational sensor features kept secret until combat in order to prevent the enemy from developing countermeasures.

name: Electromagnetic Warfare Reprogramming
description: Deliberate alteration of software, hardware, or tactics of electromagnetic warfare systems in response to changes in the enemy or the environment.
